rulings.Case Title: JOHN POPPIN VS. GEORGE V CRESSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Peter M. Radin, Jr.S Motion For Order Releasing Judgment Liens
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Friday, January 3, 2025, Line 1. DEFENDANT PETER RADIN's Motion For Order Releasing Judgment Liens. Transferred to be heard in department 301 on January 22, 2025. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/MW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE VS. DOE 1 ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Friday, January 3, 2025, Line 12. DEFENDANT DOE 1's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendant Doe 1, Big Brothers Big Sisters of America ("BBBSA") moves for summary judgment on Plaintiff's sole cause of action for negligence. In the alternative, BBBSA moves for summary adjudication on "each of the causes of action" "based on the undisputed material facts:" (1) BBBSA did not owe a duty of care to Plaintiff so there is no merit to the negligence cause of action; and, (2) BBBSA is not vicariously liable for Defendant Doe 1 ("Ramos") conduct so there is no merit to the negligence cause of action. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/MW)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. MGM DRYWALL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Business Records To Local Union 913 - Painters And Drywall Finishers
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Friday, January 3, 2025, Line 7. 2 - PLAINTIFF JANE DOE's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Business Records To Local Union 913 - Painters And Drywall Finishers. Motion granted as to Categories 1-5, 8, 9, and 12-14. Counsel for defendant is reminded that courtesy copies of all filings must be provided to the Court and counsel must comply with Local Rule 2.7B. Requests 1 through 5, 8, 9, and 12-14 are overbroad and invade Plaintiff's privacy. The Court engaged in the privacy analysis set forth in Hill v. Nat'l Collegiate Athl. Asss'n. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 1, Valley Bank of Nevada v. Superior Court (1975) 15 Cal.3d 652 and their progeny and finds that the Plaintiff's right to privacy outweighs the Defendant's right to the discovery based upon the showing made as to the specific categories identified. Although Defendant may seek discovery relating to mitigation of damages and Plaintiff's employment at Coast to Coast, the specific items which are the subject of the motion are overbroad and constitute an invasion of privacy. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/MW)

rulings.Case Title: CATALYST VENTURES II, INC. VS. JEFFREY D. LOOMIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Catalyst Ventures Ii, Inc. And To Impose Monetary Sanctions Against Catalyst Ventures Ii, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Friday, January 3, 2025, Line 2. DEFENDANT JEFFREY LOOMIS' Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Catalyst Ventures Ii, Inc. And To Impose Monetary Sanctions Against Catalyst Ventures Ii, Inc. Transferred to be heard in Department 501 on January 17, 2025 at 9:30 a.m. That department handles all landlord-tenant/real property cases per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. The moving party is immediately ordered to provide courtesy copies to Department 501 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/MW)
